reasoning_top2: The claim describes a complex three-way interaction (union effect among high-income respondents within a specific context of low-wage unionism), which typically has a low replication rate.

The reported standard errors for the two groups (0.041 and 0.040) imply a standard error of the difference around 0.057, making the 0.097 difference only marginally significant (z ~ 1.7), contradicting the claim that it is 'significant at .01%'.
